title: UNiD Spine Analyzer adverse event 1000432246-2024-00038
text: Report describes UNiD Spine Analyzer. A surgeon disagreed with information provided via Lenke classification from the software and values differed from what the software calculated. Software device analysis listed version 3.16.0.1 and determined the behavior was a software anomaly captured in CAPA PR 668690.

title: UNiD Spine Analyzer incorrect Roussouly classification
text: Report describes a software device used for spinal therapy. It states incorrect Roussouly classification for a case, with no patient involvement reported. Software device analysis identifies UNiD Spine Analyzer / SW3002 version V4.1.0 and describes software anomaly handling.

title: UNiD Spine Analyzer Barrey ratio calculation issue
text: Report describes a software device used for spinal therapy. When the image is rotated, the base value for the Barrey ratio measurement is updated even though the SVA measurement and femoral-head distance should not change. No patient involvement was reported.

title: UNiD Spine Analyzer enforcement action Z-1118-2025
text: Class II enforcement action for UNiD Spine Analyzer. Reason states software anomalies may impact rod planning and certain optional surgical parameters may have had errors resulting in incorrect calculations displayed on system, including Roussouly Classification, Real Lumbar Lordosis, Real Thoracic Kyphosis, Barrey Ratio, and Lenke Classification.

title: UNiD Spine Analyzer recall root cause
text: Recall metadata for UNiD Spine Analyzer lists software design as the root cause.

title: Lung Vision System synchronization issue
text: Report describes the Lung Vision System. The tablet and main unit were out of sync during use, creating workflow and registration concerns. This was treated as a possible software-system synchronization or registration issue.

title: Lung Vision System registration / CABT error
text: Report describes Lung Vision System false CABT error messages and registration problems during navigation. The issue may relate to software workflow, error messaging, or synchronization between components.

title: Lung Vision System hardware malfunction
text: Report describes Lung Vision System hardware malfunction and additional procedure time. The evidence is device-specific but looks more like hardware/device malfunction than AI software-output behavior.

title: Red Dot broad enforcement lead from 3M
text: Broad review-only enforcement lead containing the phrase Red Dot. Recalling firm is 3M Company - Health Care Business, not Behold.AI Technologies Limited. This should not be treated as high-confidence evidence for the Red Dot AI radiology device.

title: MAGNETOM Sola burn report
text: Report mentions MAGNETOM Sola and patient burn during an MR exam. The evidence is device-specific to an MR system but mostly physical hardware or operational MR-system safety context rather than AI model behavior.

title: AIR Recon DL broad GE MRI lead
text: Broad GE MRI adverse-event record related to OPTIMA or SIGNA MRI systems. It matched broad manufacturer/product-code context but does not specifically mention AIR Recon DL, so it should be review-only.

title: Passive adverse-event data limitation
text: Public adverse-event reports are passive reports and may be incomplete, duplicate, inaccurate, biased, unverified, and generally do not include device-use denominator data. They should not be used alone to establish incidence rates or legal causality.
